persona.name: Sarah Chen
persona.title: Senior Affiliate Manager
persona.current_tools: Impact.com, Google Sheets, manual Slack reporting
persona.personality_notes: Practical, data-driven, open to new tools if they prove ROI quickly

transcript.content: Hi Sarah, I'm [Your Name] from Refinery Affiliate. I saw you were at [Conference Name] and thought of you. We help affiliate managers like you get actionable insights from your programs directly in Slack. Would you be open to learning how?
Hi there. Interesting. What kind of insights are we talking about? I'm currently using Impact and spending a lot of time on manual reporting.

That's exactly what we help with, Sarah! Refinery can flag things like underperforming partners, potential fraud, or even new growth opportunities, all without you logging into Impact. Would a quick 10-minute chat later this week work to show you how?
Okay, that sounds like it could be helpful. I'm spending way too much time pulling reports manually. What's your availability look like Thursday or Friday afternoon?
Great! I'm free Thursday at 2 PM PST or Friday at 1 PM PST. Do either of those work for you? Happy to send a calendar invite.
Friday at 1 PM PST works for me. Please send the invite.

touchpoints.next_action: Send calendar invite for Friday at 1 PM PST.

persona.name: Jess Rodriguez
persona.title: Affiliate Manager
persona.current_tools: Impact.com, AWIN, Rakuten, multiple client dashboards
persona.personality_notes: Energetic, always looking for shortcuts, values speed over perfection

transcript.content: Hi Jess, it's [Your Name] from Refinery Affiliate. I saw you were at the recent [Conference Name] and thought of you. We're helping affiliate managers like yourself get proactive insights directly in Slack. Would you be open to a 5-min chat to see how?
Hey! Totally swamped today but "insights directly in Slack" definitely caught my eye. My team lives in Slack. What kind of insights are we talking about? Short version please! 😊
Awesome! In short, we flag revenue opportunities, margin improvements, and even potential fraud — all pushed to your Slack. When's a good time for a quick 5-min call this week to show you more?

Ooh, revenue ops and fraud detection straight into Slack sounds pretty sweet. My team is constantly drowning in client dashboards, so anything that cuts down on context switching is a win.

Can you just send me a quick bulleted list of what networks you integrate with? If it covers my main ones, then I can probably squeeze in 5 mins on Thursday afternoon.
Great! We integrate with Impact.com, CJ, AWIN, and Rakuten. If those cover your main networks, how does Thursday at 2 PM PST sound for that quick chat?
Whoa, those are literally my main networks! Okay, you got my attention. Thursday at 2 PM PST works perfectly. Send over a calendar invite! Looking forward to it.

touchpoints.next_action: Send calendar invite for Thursday at 2 PM PST

persona.name: David Park
persona.title: Director of Partnerships
pain_points: Affiliate channel is secondary to direct sales
persona.personality_notes: Analytical, needs data to be convinced, has been burned by overpromising vendors

transcript.content: Hi David, it's [Your Name] from Refinery Affiliate. I saw you were at the [Conference Name] event. We help partnership directors like you get actionable insights from their affiliate data directly in Slack. Worth a quick chat to see how?
[NO RESPONSE]

Subject: Still thinking about [Competitor Name]'s recent affiliate success, David?

Hi David,

Hope this email finds you well.

I sent a text a few days ago about how Refinery Affiliate helps partnership directors like you surface insights from your affiliate data. No worries if you missed it – things move fast!

I was doing some research on the SaaS affiliate landscape and noticed [Competitor Name]'s recent surge in [specific metric, e.g., Q3 sign-ups via affiliate channels]. While I don't have the full picture of their strategy, it immediately brought to mind how often similar opportunities are hidden within complex affiliate data.

That's precisely where Refinery Affiliate comes in. Instead of a separate dashboard, we act as an AI agent directly in your Slack, proactively analyzing your Impact.com, CJ, or other network data. Imagine getting a Slack notification that highlights, for example, underperforming affiliates compared to a competitor, or a new niche opportunity you might be missing.

No extra logins, just actionable insights pushed straight to you.

Would you be open to a quick 15-minute chat sometime this week to see a live example of how we've helped other partnership directors uncover similar
[NO RESPONSE]

touchpoints.next_action: follow up with a different value proposition or channel

transcript.content:
**(Phone rings... David answers)**

"Hi David, this is [Your Name] from Refinery Affiliate. I hope I'm not catching you at a bad time."

**(Pause for response)**

"Great. I know I've reached out a couple of times already, first by text after the [Conference Name] event, and then an email last week about how we help partnership directors like you with your affiliate data. I haven't heard back, which is totally fine – I know how busy things get. But I decided to give you a quick call because I genuinely believe what we're doing could be a game-changer for your team."

"Instead of another dashboard you have to log into, we've built an AI agent that lives right in your Slack. It connects to your affiliate networks like Impact.com or CJ, constantly sifts through your data, and then *proactively* pings you with insights. Think of it as getting a Slack notification saying, 'Hey, this affiliate is suddenly underperforming,' or 'There's a new opportunity in this niche you might be missing.' No extra work on your end, just actionable alerts."

"My main goal with this call isn'

**(Slightly annoyed tone, but professional)**

"Look, I appreciate you following up, but I'm in the middle of something pretty critical right now. I've seen your messages. We're pretty entrenched with Impact.com and some custom tools, and frankly, our affiliate channel is secondary to direct sales, so justifying *another* spend, especially for something that sounds like an alert system, is going to be tough. What's your main goal here, because I really only have a minute."

"Absolutely, David, I completely understand and I appreciate you even taking a minute when you're busy. My main goal, honestly, is to not waste your time or mine. You've hit on some really key points there – being entrenched with your current tools, and the affiliate channel often taking a backseat to direct sales."

"That's actually precisely why our users find Refinery so valuable. It's not about replacing Impact.com or your custom tools; it's about making them *more effective* without requiring more of your team's time. You mentioned justifying another spend – what if this 'alert system,' as you called it, could actually *prove* the ROI of your affiliate channel more clearly, or even just save your team hours every week that they currently spend digging for insights?"

"I don't need to dive into a full demo right now. Could I send you just one very specific example of a Slack insight we’ve delivered for a similar SaaS company – something that directly addressed either an underperforming affiliate or a missed revenue opportunity? It would take you literally 30 seconds to read. If it resonates, great. If not, then you've got your answer, and I

"Okay, I'll give you 30 seconds. Send over one *specific* example, not marketing fluff. Make sure it highlights how it integrates with Impact.com and what the actual impact was. If it's just another generic alert, we can consider this conversation done."

"Perfect, David. Thirty seconds, one specific example, no fluff. I like that. I'll make sure it's something concrete."

"What's the best email address for me to send that over to right now? I'll send it immediately after this call."
"It's david.park@yourcompany.com. And please, just that one example. No follow-up sequence, just the information I asked for."

touchpoints.key_objection: justifying another spend, affiliate channel is secondary, sounds like just an alert system
touchpoints.next_action: send specific example of Slack insight to david.park@yourcompany.com
